text.title: W3C Design Tokens Standard Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding w3c-design-tokens-standard. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every token MUST carry $type and $value; $description is required when usage is non-obvious.

rationale.source: W3C Design Tokens Community Group spec (2024 draft)

rule.rationale: Tools that consume DTCG dispatch by $type; missing $type breaks every downstream platform.

rule.statement: Tokens MUST be partitioned into primitive (raw values) and semantic (aliased intent). Primitives MUST NOT be referenced directly by component code.
rule.rationale: Direct primitive references freeze brand to specific values; semantic layer is the substitution boundary.

rule.statement: Every {alias} reference MUST resolve to an existing token. Unresolved aliases are forbidden in output.
rule.rationale: Style Dictionary builds fail or silently emit empty values on unresolved aliases.

rule.statement: Token names MUST be platform-neutral. Suffixes like '-ios', '-web' are forbidden in DTCG file; platform mapping happens downstream.
rule.rationale: Platform suffixes leak into other targets; the whole point of DTCG is platform-neutral source.

rule.statement: Color tokens used for text MUST carry a contrast self-check: pair semantic foreground tokens with background tokens and verify ≥4.5:1 (WCAG AA).
rule.rationale: Tokens that fail contrast cause downstream a11y failures that are expensive to refactor.
